key_points: Rakesh was a young boy who lived with his grandfather in Mussoorie in the Himalayan foothills
He planted a cherry seed in the garden after buying cherries from the bazaar
The cherry plant faced difficulties such as being eaten by a goat and accidentally cut by a grass cutter
Despite the damage, the plant survived and slowly grew into a strong tree
Rakesh patiently cared for the tree and was happy when it produced its first blossom
examples: Rakesh planted the cherry seed in the garden after his grandfather advised him
A goat entered the garden and ate the leaves of the young plant
A woman cutting grass accidentally cut the cherry plant with her scythe
The plant survived and new shoots began to grow again
The tree later produced a beautiful pink blossom
formulas_or_processes: Beginning → Rakesh plants a cherry seed in the garden
Challenge → The plant is damaged by animals and accidents
Growth → The plant slowly grows stronger over time
Result → The cherry tree blossoms and becomes a symbol of patience
exam_tips: Remember that Rakesh planted the cherry seed in the garden
Understand the challenges faced by the cherry tree during its growth
Focus on how patience and care helped the tree grow
Know the moral: patience and nurturing nature lead to growth and happiness
